entry.title: Re: (ABAP 7.50) SAP GUI showing label area as a blank box...
entry.content: Hi we are experiencing the same issue, the Edge web viewer is the newest version 144. does anyone have any other suggestions that i can try?

entry.title: Re: Delivery block applied at schedule line level did not...
entry.content: Whenever you create a new discussion, please try to add the related screen shots for better understanding.  When you say there were two schedule lines and delivery block is set, for which delivery date, the delivery block is set?  Sometimes, you would have set delivery block at schedule line item where the quantity is not confirmed and the next schedule line would be open and the quantity is confirmed.  So, here, system will allow to create delivery for the confirmed quantity.

entry.title: Not able to reverse combined production order when released which was created by MILL_OC.
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am prepared combination order through MILL_OC. After releasing the Combine Order all child order was get released. But due some reason I need to reverse combined order and use original order in different combined order.

But Read PP master data is grey-out. Even after TECO and deletion flag I not able to deallocate or use original order and not able to reverse release indicator.

 

 

Best Regards,

Tejas P.

entry.title: BW Modernization - Shift Infoproviders from BW PCE to DSP

entry.title: Re: Understanding Joule - (Step #4) Joule Interaction Patterns
entry.content: Very well written blog and from understanding perspective it's easy to learn.

entry.title: Re: PP-DM - Reassign the Serial Number to new production...
entry.content:
• Always verify serial number status before reassignment. Only AVLB can be reassigned.
  • Use the Serial Number assignment function in CO02 to tie them to the new order.
  • If you need mass reassignment, you can use IQ02 (Change Serial Number) to adjust equipment master linkage before assigning in the order.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere (DSP) and SAC data and metadata versioning / backup and restore
entry.content:
Dear Martin, thanks for your insightful blog (series). I have seen many companies using the Content Network 'incorrectly' as a kind of storage for versioning when it comes to business self‑service, both in DSP and SAC. For example, they create a transport package with a specific version of the objects and simply leave it in the Content Network as a form of backup. What are your thoughts on this and how can this behavior be avoided?

For the next reader: In the meantime, SAC now offers story versioning in a way that is equivalent to DSP with the latest update (except you actively need to create a version instead of automated version creation with a deployment).

entry.title: Re: C4C Workflow Calculation for opportunities not working
entry.content: Hi Fernando, this is working! Thanks a lot! Best regards, Markus

entry.title: Stock Transfer Order Delivery completed indicator
entry.content:
Scenario/ Business requirement
We use Stock Transport Orders (STO) to transfer stock from one plant to another (intra/intercompany). Sometimes the supplying plant cannot issue the full quantity in the first outbound delivery. Example: The STO is for 1,000 EA, but the supplying plant issues 900 EA (100 short). When the receiving plant posts the GR for 900 EA, the Delivery Completed indicator (ELIKZ) on the STO item remains open, and users must manually close the item.

Goal
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, we want an automatic way to close such STO items—e.g., if the remaining quantity has not been delivered within 7 days, automatically set Delivery Completed (ELIKZ) on the STO item so it stops appearing in open order backlogs.

 

We read SAP Note 1093582 (“Delivery completed indicator (ELIKZ)”) but there is no solution for SAP PUBLIC CLOUD in the note. Is there any possibility to configure this in Public Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Manage KPIs and Reports - CDS View contains parameter P_GLAccountHierarchy with / (slash)
entry.content:
Hello Fernando,

i don´t think it has something to do with replication. This transaction is used to replicate hierarchies from old R/3 tables to new S/4 tables. I can´t select these hierarchies: H001 or "H003" there.

Table HRRP_DIRECTORY and table HRRP_NODE seems normal.

I don´t think the issue is in the table.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Proxy generation support with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Prasanthi,

The Constraint is only design-time, not runtime.Existing SAP PO–proxy-based interfaces can remain  fully functional at runtime until you switch to point to CPI runtime endpoint

Before switching the SM59 destination to point to Integration Suite, ensure all existing PO-based proxies are:

  • Activated

  • Transported to QA/Prod

  • Not expected to change structurally anymore

As a next step do the switch to the Integration Suite and further configuration of logical ports and destinations with CPI end points once you have the corresponding ICO migrated or an Iflow configured.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: CAMT Import Error: Message About Old GVCs Despite New BTC Configuration (OT83)
entry.content:
Dear Community,
We have set up CAMT for a customer, including the new BTC codes. However, when the file is imported, the account statement is not posted because it displays a message stating that the old GVCs and the external transaction code UNALLOCATED are not maintained.

However, this cannot be the case because the new BTCs are maintained, as is the Unallocated code.
It also pulls the new transaction type. This is already stored in the bank accounts. This concerns the old GVCs 166 and 804.
The entire setup was done in transaction OT83.

Nevertheless, the following message appears:

Has anyone had this problem and knows what could be causing it?

Thank you.
Best Regards, 
Vanessa

#onprem #camt #gvc

entry.title: Translation Functionalities
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am in the process of doing the Learning Journey for SEN but at the same time, I'm assisting a client with Training Content Development. (They are new users of SEN).
For Demonstration purposes only, I created a short course as a Proof of Context for P2P module in English.  Now I need to translate this short course into a different language to show the translation capabilities.

- The option to change the language of the Simulation works, but the context in the book pages do not translate of the project.

- I performed the task to export and import the XLF file from the root folder, after selecting the language. But when I import, the system displays a message "No Target Language" defined.

- The Language field in Basic Properties under the UID, has been set to the preferred language.

 

1. What configuration is required to translate the project with both the sims and book pages?

2. What config is required for the Short description and Long Description in the Basic Properties to translate?

3. When the Project has been translated, should the document outputs (compound/Training document) not also convert automatically?

4. As this is only for demonstration purposes, the SAP Translation License Hub cannot be an option right now. However, I think our current SEN client platform does include SAP Translation support. Not sure.

5. Where in the "Implementing SAP Enable Now" Learning Course can I find more detail on the Translations?

 

See screenshots of steps:
1.  

 

2. 

3. 

4.

entry.title: Re: Material Ledger with or without Actual Costing
entry.content:
1. Price Control ‘S’ + Price Determination ‘2’
Price Control ‘S’ (Standard Price) is required if you want ML to calculate variances and revaluate stock. ML works only with S materials; V (MAP) materials are not revaluated in ML. They remain at moving average price and don’t participate in actual costing.

Price Determination ‘2’ (Transaction-based) is the correct setting for ML with actual costing. It ensures that all procurement transactions (GRs, invoices, etc.) are captured in ML for variance calculation. So yes, S + 2 is the right combination for ML evaluation.

2. Is Actual Costing Mandatory?
You can activate ML without actual costing, just for currency/valuation purposes (e.g., parallel currencies, group valuation). In this case, ML collects values but does not calculate a periodic actual price.

If your goal is to release the calculated periodic price into the next period, then Actual Costing is mandatory. Actual costing (CKMLCP) performs the period-end closing, rolls up variances, and determines the Periodic Unit Price (PUP). The dynamic price release function then updates the standard price for the next period based on the PUP.

So what this means for your case

  • Since you want to release the standard price automatically from one period to the next, you need Actual Costing active.
  • Without actual costing, ML will not calculate a new periodic price — it will just carry forward the old standard price.
  • MAP (‘V’) materials will not be part of ML actual costing; only S-controlled materials are evaluated.

entry.title: Re: Lookup table data price is not multiplied for quarter...
entry.content:
Dear Elena, 

please check out the section about billing cycle depandant prices in the Pricing Configuration Guide .
In short, you have to use another input column with field periodicity in the lookup table which stores the price.

There are alternative ways:
- you could be to build your pricing logic to factor in the periodicity length. To do so, you can 
retrieve a factor for all billing cycles from another lookup table, e.g. for anniversary-monthly the factor is 1, for anniversary-quarterly it is 3. 
After this, you then calculate an effective factor by multiplying this factor with the field prorata and use this in the Calculate price element statement as the ratio. 
- the duration component allows to calculate the duration from billingCycleStart to billingCycleEnd 
The first approach allows you to define different prices periodicity, e.g. a monthly plan has higher prices compared to a yearly plan. Like in the previous approach you would then use this to calculate the effective factor in a similar way. You need to consider that Subscription Billing also supports weekly billing cycles. 

Personally, I would recommend the way described in the guide, unless it is really a pure linear correlation of the price with the number of months. 

Kind regards,
Carsten

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add a new plant to a company code i...
entry.content:
Dear @Wayne10,

Yes adding a new plant is technically allowed in the Starter System, but not by manual customizing directly in the S/4HANA tenant the way you might expect from On-Prem. In the Public Cloud Starter System the organizational structure is driven from SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) where it is prefilled to match SAP Best Practices / test scripts

What SAP states for the Starter System:

  • The Starter System comes with a preconfigured organizational structure
  • You can enhance that preconfigured structure by creating your own organizational units this includes org elements like plants
  • To make newly created org units available in the target system, you must confirm them otherwise they won’t be deployed
  • SAP also highlights that while you can add org entities, it’s not recommended in the Starter System because it’s meant to align closely with the Best Practices test scripts used in Fit-to-Standard workshops

IMPORTANT: No demo configuration or master data is delivered for the additional org units you create so you’ll have to maintain required config/master data yourself for meaningful testing

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/deploying-content-in-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-starter-system

Practical implication:

  • Create a new plant via CBC in Organizational Structure

 

 

  • Not the intended approach: Trying to create it manually only inside the Starter System tenant because CBC is the governing layer and the starter content is designed to stay aligned with scripts

Recommendation for testing:

  • If your goal is quick testing it’s usually easier to reuse an existing plant in the starter content
  • If your goal is test my own org design, that’s typically better done in the implementation/development configuration tenants rather than bending the Starter System away from the delivered Best Practices structure

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add a new plant to a company code i...
entry.content:
Hi Dawid,

I am familiar with how to edit the organizational structure. I raised the question because, in CBC, the org structure currently has the status “Completed,” which prevents me from making any changes to it at this stage.

entry.title: Re: Extract a measure depending on a max measure
entry.content:
Hello @FrancescoPetrocelli ,

Here is a solution in Datasphere (Graphical View). Hope this helps 🙂 
You can use a "Window" function, which is something Datasphere dosen't really tell you - but you can read more here. 

You'll want to use the MAX() function, over your two dimensions, "Centro" and "Codice Materiale", like this:

MAX(qta colli) OVER (PARTITION by centro, CodiceMaterial) -- Remember to change to the correct column names.

This will give you the max. See my example below:

After you've done that, you can wrap your MAX in a case statement, like this:

case 
    when qta colli = MAX(qta colli) OVER (PARTITION by Centro, Codice Materiale) then 
        tempo lordo lavorato
    else 
        0
    end

See my example:

That should do the trick 🙂 

Note: You might need to add "Period of analysis" to the Partition clause, so it does a MAX within the reporting period, but you can experiment a bit and see what clicks.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: How to change bank account on the invoice form in SAP Public Cloud

I have a problem with the bank account on the invoice output. The invoice output only shows one specific bank account, even if this account is deactivated in the Manage Bank Accounts app. I set up a different bank account number in the same app that I want to show on the invoice form, but the change didn't show up in the output.
Could you please clarify where exactly I can specify or activate the bank account to be used for the invoice? Alternatively, what settings are required for this?

Thank you in advance for your help.

I can see the DataServices element in the V2 XML and everything looks fine.  Is this a bug in the current version of the BTP ABAP Environment or has this functionality been disabled (to stop people using this blog 😀)

Andrew

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to add a new plant to a company code i...
entry.content:
Dear @Wayne10,

Thank you for the clarification.

In SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) the ability to use Edit is restricted to higher roles. The Key User role explicitly does not allow critical actions like changes via Edit

So if you’re assigned as:

  • Key user (SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_KEY_USER) -> Edit is disabled
  • Project manager / Program manager (SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_PROJECT_LEAD / SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_PROGRAM_LEAD) -> Edit is available within the workspaces you’re assigned to

More details below, also I put table from link:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/administrating-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/introduction-sap-central-business-configuration

What to do:

  1. Ask your CBC admin / IT contact / program manager to assign you a role that allows Changes via Edit and make sure you’re assigned to the relevant workspace/team
  2. Then you can reopen Set Up Organizational Structure with Edit and create an additional plant and confirm it if you want it deployed

For example assigned role you will find in Settings:

 

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP RAP - fields of root and child on one object page
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I have a simple managed RAP-Scenarion with a BO containing the follwing Entities:

  • Root entity (e.g. Invoice)
  • One Child Entity  (e.g. Position)
  • Cardianality is [1..*]

When I start the Application, I can click on create and enter the Data for the Root-Node. After that, I can click on create again and entert he data fort he child. So far, so good. This is the standard behavior the RAP Framework offers.

 

I’m wondering if it is possible, to create a managed scenarion with an Object Page, where all Fields of the Root and the Child are displayed all at once.

I found a solution here https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/create-by-association-for-1-1-composition-in-rap-unmanaged/qaq-p/14006338. But after implementing, the fields of the child are shown in a second facet on the same screen as the root entity. But the fields are not editable. I think this is because of the cardinality I used in my example. The cardinality in the mentioned link between root and child is 0..1.

 

Thank you for any hints, tricks …

 

BR

 

Peter

entry.title: Talent intelligence hub transformation in practice
entry.content:
Join HR Transformation Advisor Nicole Wright and Solution Value Advisor Rebecca Reagan-Thieme to learn how to plan and execute change management to support your organization's skills transformation journey.

Register here: https://sap-se.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_HV5f52-jT8ON4DaJhtvzxw

entry.title: SAP Business Network on supporting regulatory processing of e-invoices in France
entry.content:
Countries across Europe are steadily rolling out e-Invoicing regulations to close the VAT gap (see the recent cases of Belgium and Poland), and France is the next country in that journey. Initially planned for July 1, 2024, France’s mandate has been postponed to September 2026. From that date, electronic invoice issuance and e-reporting will be mandatory for large and medium-sized companies, while receiving e-invoices will become mandatory for all companies regardless of size. This phased approach aims to improve fiscal transparency and automation while giving organizations time to adapt their systems and processes.

Technically, the mandate requires businesses to issue, send, and receive domestic electronic invoices in one of the several formats compliant with the European Norm: UBL, CII, or Factur-X. France also introduces an invoice life cycle concept via standardized invoice statuses, enabling end-to-end visibility and reconciliation for all trading partners. Both supplier and buyer are required to use a private certified platform, referred to as an Plateforme agréée PA. The invoice mandate only applies to domestic invoices, meaning that intra-EU or cross border transactions won’t fall under the mandate.

SAP Business Network as Plateforme agréée

SAP holds official accreditation as a Plateforme agréée (PA), reinforcing its position as a trusted partner for compliant e-invoicing and e-reporting solutions in France. 

For trading partners, SAP Business Network will support regulatory compliance by converting invoices to the mandated format (UBL) in accordance with the European norm, and by enabling seamless connectivity with the tax portal PPF (Portail Public de Facturation) as well as the buyer’s accredited Plateforme agréée (PA).

This approach is designed to deliver reliable invoice exchange, end-to-end visibility, and alignment with regulatory obligations while minimizing operational complexity for suppliers.

SAP Business Network's mandate support will be available by the end of May 2026, leaving ample time for testing, preparing and confidently starting compliance with the mandate requirements.

Further communications and detailed guidance will be issued in the coming weeks and months, delivering actionable steps to support stakeholder’s readiness and operational planning ahead of the phased implementation of France’s e-invoicing and e-reporting requirements. 

 

Stay informed by subscribing to the tag "SAP Business Network for Suppliers" and by regularly visiting our page.

entry.title: Re: Change Management (PLM-CR) Vs Engineering Change Mana...

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 - Bin Setup
entry.content:
Hi,

you need to assing bin locations to a Pick list line when creating the document. It is however possible to change the allocation before completing the picking process (e.g. Ctrl+Tab on the Quantity if the item is batch managed).

As for having the information on all available locations for that item/batch, for this you will need to create a custom print layout.

BR,

Matija

Hello @manoj_yadav60 ,

Creating a post method on CL_BS_BP_OVP_ASSIST->IF_FPM_OVP_CONF_EXIT~OVERRIDE_EVENT_OVP  is one common way to implement a framework for handling visibility of UIBBs. Please check this question and answers of SAP Community: Show/Hide and/or Enable/Disable UIBB FORM

entry.title: Re: Additional Balance Posted to Fixed Asset After Partia...
entry.content:
What you are seeing is a parallel valuation issue in Asset Accounting. The extra posting with transaction type 331 “Proportional Cumulative Revaluation on Replacement Value” is not coming from CJ8G itself, but from the valuation logic in your second ledger (parallel valuation / replacement value ledger).

Why the difference occurs is because of the following:

  • In leading ledger, the settlement posts exactly the AuC balance (30,010,205.45).
  • In the second ledger, the system applies replacement value revaluation rules.
  • When you partially capitalize, the system interprets this as a proportional transfer of replacement value, triggering transaction type 331.
  • That’s why you see the additional 1,721,920.47 posted;  it is the proportional revaluation component.

Why it didn’t happen before is because, in your earlier case, the system used transaction type 336 (partial capitalization without revaluation). The difference is usually due to:

  • Asset master data settings (valuation areas, revaluation flag).
  • Ledger group assignment (which ledger is active for revaluation).
  • Settlement rule target asset (different asset classes or valuation settings can trigger different transaction types).

How to avoid triggering revaluation is by checking the Asset Master Data. In the receiver asset, review the valuation areas and whether “Revaluation on Replacement Value” is active. If you don’t want proportional revaluation, remove that setting for the asset class/valuation area.

Ensure the settlement is restricted to the leading ledger only, if you don’t want postings in the revaluation ledger. Alternatively, adjust the ledger group customizing so that revaluation is not triggered for this transaction type.

In OAOA / AO90 (transaction type assignment), check how transaction type 331 is mapped for your valuation areas. You can adjust the customizing so that partial capitalization uses 336 instead of 331 in the second ledger.

On the Process recommendation side, if you want pure capitalization without revaluation, use a receiver asset that does not have replacement value revaluation active. Or configure the valuation area so that proportional revaluation is not calculated during CJ8G settlement.

entry.title: Re: Copy Purchase Requisition(PR) price to Purchase Order(PO)

entry.title: ABAP RAP - Action is not Updating the UI (unmanaged / managed)
entry.content:
Hello Community

I am currently trying to fill a field with a different value using an action. To do this, I create a READ entity to determine the selected rows, then I change the field and return it using the Result statement.

Unfortunately, the UI is not updated so that the new value appears in the column.

I have stored my action as follows:
action load result [1] $self;

My action method looks like this:

READ ENTITIES OF XXXX IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY YYYYY
FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
RESULT DATA(lt_rows)
FAILED failed.

LOOP AT lt_rows ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<row>).
<row>-FIELD1 = ‘NEW_TEXT’.
<row>-FIELD2 = ‘NEW TEXT 2’.
INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <row>-%tky %param = <row> ) INTO TABLE result.
ENDLOOP.

Regardless of whether I try it in a managed or unmanaged environment, it does not work. Could someone assist me in identifying what the problem might be?

Current system is SAP S4HANA 2023 FP03.

Best regards, Wolfgang

entry.title: New SAP Learning Insights podcast episode with Stefanos’ Finance Update is live now!
entry.content:
In this episode, David Chaviano met again with Stefanos Pougkas from SAP to talk about the evolution of SAP's financial offerings. They explore the core functions of SAP Financials, the shift to SAP Public Cloud solutions, and the importance of foundational accounting knowledge for newcomers. Stefanos highlights the impact of AI on automating financial processes and encourages embracing a fit-to-standard philosophy in cloud environments. We conclude by discussing the future of finance consultants amidst evolving market demands, offering valuable insights for anyone looking to excel in SAP finance. And of course, at the end of the episode, Stefanos shares some words of wisdom.

Download transcript

Dive deeper

  • Exploring Accounting & Financial Close
  • Applying End-to-End Business Processes inSAP Business Suite: 
  • SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant- End-to-End Business Processes for SAP Business Suite
  • SAP Learning site – Performance-based Certification
  • SAP Community blog: Reimagining Certification for the Way We Work Today
  • Learn by products - Financial Management

Further resources

  • Visit SAP Learning 
  • Join the SAP Learning Group
  • Visit the SAP Community 

Enjoy listening to this new podcast! 

Apple          Spotify

entry.title: Re: How To reset SAP HANA Cloud Connector(SCC)'s built-in user(e.g. Administrator) password
entry.content:
we have a cloud based S4hana system and Cloud connector was delivered already installed. meaning we dont have OS level Access? how do I  change/reset the password then?
we are using the "SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance". 

need urgent help thanks. 

Rashed Popalzai

entry.title: Re: How to change bank account on the invoice form in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi ,

the “Partner Bank Type” field in the  document is one of the key places that controls which bank account appears on the invoice output.

Go to invoice change mode , there is filed called Partner bank drop down and select correct bank account then save

entry.title: Re: RF in Datasphere Unable to fetch delta data Server re...
entry.content:
Hi All,

When you receive the following error message: “Unable to fetch delta data from source, although data is available. Server resources may be overloaded.”

Please check the DHCDCMON transaction in S/4. This transaction allows you to monitor all CDC replication flows. In this transaction you can check the Subscriber Worklist. If any entries are present, this indicates an issue with the replication.

Select the appropriate subscriber ID and restart the status. Once restarted, the delta data should be picked up successfully in SAP Datasphere.

Blog CDC Monitor: https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/datasphere/4549335823.html?locale=en-US 

 

Good luck!

Marinke

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Hamburg
entry.content:
Es sind auch alle SAP Interessierten zum Austausch willkommen, die vielleicht nicht bei den DSAG - TT dabei sind. 
Wir können dann noch mal über die aktuellen offenen Fragen diskutieren.

entry.title: Manage Situation Types and Manage Teams and Responsibilities app
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to use "Manage Situation Type" app for the very first time.
With the help of the blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/situation-handling-how-to-configure-and-trigger-situations/ba-p/12855788 i started the set up.

I used existing standard Situation Handling template.
I realized in order to add Notification receipients, I needs to do some setup using "Manage Teams and Responsibility" app.

While creating a Team and adding Team Owners and Team Members, I understood I am not able to search any Business Partners there.

I tried to create a Business partner with role BUP003, but I do not see role BUP003 in a dropdown while creating BP.
(I am on On premise, 1909 version)

1. Is it necessary to have Employee Master created first in SAP HCM, and then only BP can be created with BUP003 role?
2. Does that mean SAP HCM set up is needed in order to use "Manage Teams and Responsibilities" and "manage Situation Type" app?

What am I missing here?

Please help.

Thanks,
Dhananjay

Any idea how to reset the Password, when we dont have OS level access? 

we need urgent help. Thanks, 

Rashed Popalzai

entry.title: Re: SAP GRC AC Ruleset for SAP ACM

entry.title: Re: Can’t create report based on I_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEMCUBE - CDS view not whitelisted
entry.content: Have find any solution for this? I am also facing the same issue for a different custom CUBE which we created

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public – Standard Approach for OPEX Budgeting & Control
entry.content:
We would like to seek clarification on SAP’s official and recommended standard approach for managing OPEX budgeting and expenditure control in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Specifically, we would appreciate confirmation on the following:

  1. SAP-Recommended Standard Approach for OPEX
  • What is SAP’s officially recommended approach to manage OPEX budgeting and control in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition today?
  1. Supported Functional Capabilities
    Is there standard, supported functionality available for:
  • Budget availability control
  • Budget checks during Purchase Requisition (PR) and Purchase Order (PO) processing
  • Periodic budget consumption and monitoring reports
  • Budget governance and approval controls
  • Spending control mechanisms
  • Budget control for non-project OPEX expenses without using Project Systems (PS)

This clarification is critical for us, as OPEX budgeting and control is a core financial governance requirement. We want to ensure our implementation:

  • Remains aligned with SAP standard and recommended practices
  • Avoids unnecessary complexity or custom development
  • Supports sustainable operations post go-live

Any guidance or confirmation from SAP or community experts would be greatly appreciated

regards

entry.title: How to get the query of a dataprovider. BO 4.3
entry.content:
Hello.

How to get the text query of a dataproviders by REST API.

For version 4.2 query could be obtained 

{0}/raylight/v1/documents/{1}/dataproviders/{2}

As a temporary solution, I am receiving queryplan. 

{0}/raylight/v1/documents/{1}/dataproviders/{2}/queryplan

But, query and queryplan are not the same thing

entry.title: Re: serialisation requirement missing in EWM inbound delivery
entry.content: For me I created new material and assigned the new serial number profile after full configuration its working fine.

entry.title: How to A/B test, track winners, and auto-reuse winning emails in Emarsys automation program?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m working on implementing A/B testing within Emarsys automation program and need guidance on the following:

  • How to set up A/B testing within automation programs to support different email variants.
  • How to track and determine the winning variant based on performance metrics during or after the campaign.
  • How to automate the reuse of the winning email variant in subsequent campaigns or automation programs without manual intervention.

Is there a native way in Emarsys to automate winner selection and dynamically update future campaigns or program emails with the winning variant? If not, what are the best recommended workarounds?

Note: Client wants to set this up within the automation program so that winning variants are automatically sent to next incoming contacts in program.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to unlock/remove application user locks in SAP S/...
entry.content: Thank you Chris, but my concern is regarding user transaction or posting lock by another user or by self. How can we terminate the other user. I have attached a screenshot for reference

entry.title: Period Lock Despite Correct MM Config
entry.content:
Help needed! I’m configuring a new Pharma enterprise structure (Company PH01) and hit a stubborn period error during initial stock posting (Mvt 561). Standard MM gates are open, but the transaction is blocked.

The Setup:

  • Company Code: PH01 (V-Pharma India)

  • Fiscal Year Variant: V3 (April-March)

  • Current Action: Posting 1,000 units of V-PARA-500 (Material Type ZMDG)

Audit Trail (Validated):

  • OMSY: Initialized to Period 11 / 2026.

  • MMRV: Confirmed Period 11 is current; back-posting to 10 is allowed.

  • MMPV: Log confirms CoCd is already at 11/2026.

  • MIGO Dates: Both Posting and Document dates set to today (03.02.2026).

  • Line Item: "Item OK" checked; Plant/SLoc PH01 recognized.

The Conflict: Despite MMRV showing Period 11 is open, MIGO returns M7053: "Posting only possible in periods 2026/11 and 2026/10."

entry.title: Re: Manage KPIs and Reports - CDS View contains parameter P_GLAccountHierarchy with / (slash)
entry.content:
Hi David,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's what the CDS view expert replied:

Question1 : Please try to add a parameter P_GLAccountHierarchy in their custom query, rather than pass hard coding value ‘'H109/HONE/H003'. It should like as select from I_MargAnlysProdAndSrvcCube(P_GLAccountHierarchy :$parameters.P_GLAccountHierarchy).

Question2: define transient view entity XXXX  provider contract analytical_query can replace @analytics.query: true if customer’s sap version is supported. And @odata.publish: true is optional not mandatory.

I hope that helps.

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: How can i restrict access to custom CDS View Data by Access Control on PFCG in S4HPC
entry.content:
Hi.

I need to restrict access to CDS View Data by Access Control on PFCG in S4HPC.

How can i link Authorization Object with Business Role?

I've created custom CDS View

Also Access Control on PFCG authorization

I also created custom Authorization Object / Field ZVHAOBJ1 / ZVHFLD1. 

Now question: how can i assign Authorization Object ZVHAOBJ1 to particular Business Role to restrict access to CDS View Data for users who DO NOT have this authorization. 

Regards

Vitali

entry.title: SAP POs to Ariba B&I without Commerce Automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I wanted to check if there is a way that we can send Purchase Orders Originated from SAP S/4 HANA to SAP Ariba B&I without Commerce Automation License? Has anyone done this? I am aware that with Commerce automation license we will be able to do a PO Copy from Network to SAP B&I for POs originating in S/4 or ECC.

We have SAP S/4 HANA where POs will be created, we don't have Commerce Automation license and we have SAP Ariba B&I Solution (Suite Integrated). We can't move our Ordering to Ariba B&I for some reason, but we would like the PO created in SAP S/4 to be available in Ariba B&I as a copy and at the same time if Network can send this PO to Supplier.

Please confirm if this is feasible at all if we have BTP license with us.

Thanks,
Velu

entry.title: Authorization activities before and after SAP S/4HANA On-Premise upgrade (SAP GUI only)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA On-Premise upgrade project (SAP S/4HANA 2021 to SAP S/4HANA 2023) and I am responsible for Authorization. I would like to ask for best practices and recommendations regarding authorization activities before and after the upgrade.

  • Before the S/4HANA upgrade, what authorization-related checks or preparation activities should be done?
  • After the S/4HANA upgrade, which authorization follow-on activities are mandatory?

Please help to advise

Thanks,
QuocDT5

entry.title: 12 characters limit on UserID in SU01
entry.content:
We are not able increase the 12 character limit on UserID created in SU01. it is not accepting UserID longer than 12 characters.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 12 characters limit on UserID in SU01
entry.content:
The user name in SU01 is stored in table USR02 at field BNAME which is defined by data element XUBNAME, CHAR 12. It is used by the Kernel and may not be modified. This is SAP standard and used in many applications. SAP does not support the extension of user ID.

Please read the KBA below in this regards:

1930155 - Is it possible to extend user name length to more than 12 characters or the Alias length to more than 40 characters in SU01

entry.title: Release Navigator for Customer Experience – Q1 2026 Update
entry.content:
Hello CX Community! 

As we begin another year of innovation across the Customer Experience (CX) portfolio, we are excited to share that the release navigator for Customer Experience is now complete for Q1 2026.  

With every new release, a wide range of information, documentation, and resources are updated and made available for you to review. Our goal is to provide timely enablement to help users get ready for the latest release cycle. 

 

Tool Overview

If you are not yet familiar with the release navigator for Customer Experience, it is a resource provided by SAP Enterprise Support designed to offer the following: 

  • A central repository for current product information 
  • A unified access point for all customer experience release materials, including documentation, blogs, webinars, schedules, e-learnings and newsletters 
  • Streamlined access to release details across SAP solutions through additional release navigators 

Content Insights 

Q1 brings updated release information across SAP Sales Cloud, SAP Service Cloud, SAP Engagement Cloud previously known as SAP Emarsys, and SAP Marketing Cloud.  Products following a continuous delivery model: SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 and SAP Commerce Cloud also feature updated Q4 recap content.  

Several important updates have been introduced in this quarter’s release navigator.  

New Release Blog Posts for SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 

New release blog posts are available for SAP Sales and SAP Service Cloud Version 2. These blogs provide a concise summary of the most significant enhancements delivered each quarter. They are designed to give teams a quick and comprehensive overview of the latest updates. 

Enhancements to the General Page 

The General page within the release navigator has been expanded to include new content. Notably, the SAP Enterprise Support Academy and the CX webcast page have been added, offering users enhanced access to educational resources and support materials directly through the navigator. 

Changes to SAP Marketing Cloud  

Release webcasts for SAP Marketing Cloud will be discontinued as this product is in the process of being retired. Despite this change, SAP remains committed to supporting customers during the transition period by continuing to provide minor enhancement updates. 

Innovation Schedule 

Following the established rhythm from previous years, the release navigator will continue to release content updates quarterly ahead of the planned CX product releases in February, May, August, and November. This ensures you always have the latest information at your fingertips to keep projects running smoothly.  

In addition, new user- experience updates will follow a single annual update cycle, delivered in August along with the Q3 content update. This approach supports a stable and predictable evolution of the platform’s look and feel.  

Informational Timeline: 

 Disclaimer: These dates are for informational purposes only and may change. The Readiness@Scale team has the final decision over any UX modifications.

Connect with Us

Your feedback is essential to shaping future improvements to the release navigator. We invite you to:

  • Complete our feedback survey
  • Share ideas or requests in the comments section
  • Learn more about the Readiness@Scale team
  • Explore additional content

Thank you for your continued engagement and support.

Best Run,
Readiness@Scale

SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Re: Posting Period Set Wrongly
entry.content:
Please provide additional information, including :

  • Details on how the posting periods were configured, such as the specific Customizing transactions used (e.g., OB52, OB29, etc.).
  • The types of documents that have already been posted (e.g., pure financial, materia stock, assets, etc.).
  • Confirmation whether all documents posted in January can be reversed (before)  and reposted (after the Customizing changes are made), especially if they are not purely FI documents so reverse in the original application (MM, SD, etc.)

But pls aware that since HANA 2.0 SPS7 LSS should be used in many cases. (S/4 for example)

Recommendations for Data Encryption | SAP Help Portal

its recommended to use LSS -> it it would be great if you add the LSS part here too, 

Thank you very much

entry.title: INVENTORY VALUTION AT COMPANY CODE LEVEL

Dear Experts,

Best Regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: EWM Pick by WO influence source and destination
entry.content:
I would like to create a WO per storage type so that one WO can contain multiple items. Picking should be done via the RF process “Picking by WO” (non‑HU managed).

The standard picking behavior, for example with three WTs in one WO, is:

  • Confirm source for the first WT
  • Confirm source for the second WT
  • Confirm source for the third WT
  • Then confirm all destinations (first, second, third)

Is it possible to change this sequence so that it works as follows instead?

  • Confirm source for the first WT → confirm destination for the first WT
  • Confirm source for the second WT → confirm destination for the second WT
  • Confirm source for the third WT → confirm destination for the third WT

The only option I found that works is to create one WO per WT; however, I need to keep multiple WTs within a single WO.

Please advise

entry.title: Re: About task Create Project Schedule and Budget n the i...
entry.content:
Dear @Amy_Astudillo,

You’re reading it correctly in SAP Cloud ALM, when you create the project, pick the Activate roadmap and set phase dates, you’ve created a high-level timeline. But that’s not yet the same thing as a baselined project schedule + budget in the project-management sense the roadmap task is talking about

Here’s why that roadmap task still exists and what it’s really trying to achieve.

When you select an SAP Activate roadmap in Cloud ALM, you get an actionable task list and you can plan with phases / sprints / milestones and visualize tasks in a Gantt chart

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/gantt-chart-view

That’s a strong start but it’s intentionally template-driven and content-driven:

  • It loads standard roadmap tasks generic to many projects
  • Phase dates are essentially timeboxes containers. They don’t automatically produce a WBS-level network with real dependencies, resource commitments, critical path, etc.

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/solution-transformation-consultant-sap-cloud-alm-delta/running-successful-projects-in-sap-cloud-alm

What the roadmap task Create Project Schedule and Budget is really asking for? That task is a project governance requirement before we start execution, agree on the plan and the money, and baseline them

In practice, it means you must explicitly create and get approval for:

1. A detailed schedule (WBS + dependencies + resources) not just phase dates

A real schedule typically includes things that your initial ALM setup doesn’t automatically define:

  • decomposed work packages per workstream business, data, integrations, security, testing, cutover
  • effort + duration estimates
  • explicit task dependencies so you can see critical path / impacts
  • named resource assignments not just roles and time commitments
  • constraints for example cannot start before fit-to-standard sign-off

Cloud ALM can support much of this once you refine tasks. Gantt view lets you maintain durations and relationships, it also shows effort/story points but you still have to do the planning work it’s not created just by setting phase dates

2. A budget baseline

A project budget is usually more than effort hours on tasks, it includes:

  • implementation partner costs
  • internal backfill
  • training/change/adoption costs
  • tooling testing, migration, integrations, environments, data activities, travel, etc.

Cloud ALM is primarily an ALM/project execution tool, budgeting is often owned by PMO/Finance tooling and processes cost baseline, approvals, change control. The roadmap task exists because every project still needs that baseline, regardless of the execution tool you use

3. Release/upgrade windows and transport freezes must be planned explicitly in Public Cloud

For Public Cloud upgrades are part of life. SAP own onboarding guidance calls out adjusting the plan for releases and avoiding go-lives around upgrade/transport restriction windows. Cloud ALM also highlights release/upgrade windows in its cross-project timeline views, but you still need to incorporate that into your schedule baseline

So do you need a second schedule outside Cloud ALM?

Not necessarily. A better way to interpret the roadmap task is:

  • Ensure a schedule and budget baseline exist and are approved
  • Cloud ALM can be where you maintain the schedule but phase dates alone are not enough

Practical takeaway

The roadmap task exists because SAP Activate is tool-agnostic and is ensuring you don’t confuse:

  • timeboxed phases high-level timeline
    with
  • a baselined schedule + budget governance artifacts used to control scope/time/cost

I hope that my answer will help you

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Sap DataSphere local table partition size
entry.content: 0.6 billions records per partition as Hash partition divides the dataset equally among the partitions.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the Future: The 2H 2025 Home Page Redesign in SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hello, is there road map info, i.e. when does this Latest Home Page become opt-out or universal?

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/317660

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/344248

They are adding events to C1 released interface BOs like I_SalesOrderTP and I_productTP_2.

in newer versions(2025) we can conusme events from these(rather than consuming from unreleased BOs like R_SalesOrderTP) and these will be considered clean core.

entry.title: Re: How to Use Custom Fields in Sales Document BAPIs
entry.content:
Hi @CE6, We have added several fields which includes date fields using 'Custom fields' in VBAK. Now we are trying to update the same using BAPI 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE'.  Getting error 'Please enter correct date' from BAPI. Below is the code reference.

DATA: ls_header        TYPE bapisdh1,
        ls_headerx       TYPE bapisdh1x,
        lt_extensionin   TYPE TABLE OF bapiparex,
        ls_extensionin   TYPE bapiparex,
        ls_bape_vbak     TYPE bape_vbak,
        ls_bape_vbakx    TYPE bape_vbakx,
        ls_header_fields TYPE zsds_sales_booking_change.

  DATA lt_bapiparex    TYPE bapiparextab.
  DATA lr_header_ext   TYPE REF TO bape_sdsalesdoc.
  DATA lo_bapi_mapping TYPE REF TO if_cfd_bapi_mapping.
  DATA ls_order_header_inx TYPE bapisdh1x.

  ls_header_fields = im_booking_change.

  " Fill header custom fields
  CLEAR ls_order_header_inx.
  ls_order_header_inx-updateflag = 'U'.

  CREATE DATA lr_header_ext.

  IF ls_header_fields-zz_ocdt IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_ocdt_sdh = sy-datum."ls_header_fields-zz_ocdt.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_ocdt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

IF ls_header_fields-zz_qacertificatedt IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_qacertificatedt_sdh = sy-datum."ls_header_fields-zz_qacertificatedt.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_qacertificatedt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

  IF ls_header_fields-zz_agreementregno IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_agreementsdt_sdh = ls_header_fields-zz_agreementregno.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_agreementsdt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

  IF ls_header_fields-zz_booking_form_dt IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_booking_form_dt_sdh = sy-datum."ls_header_fields-  zz_booking_form_dt.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_booking_form_dt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

" Do header custom fields mapping
  lo_bapi_mapping = cl_cfd_bapi_mapping=>get_instance( ).

  TRY.
      lo_bapi_mapping->map_to_bapiparex_single( EXPORTING ir_source_structure = lr_header_ext
                                                CHANGING  ct_bapiparex        = lt_bapiparex ).
    CATCH cx_cfd_bapi_mapping INTO DATA(lx_cfd_bapi_mapping_error).
  ENDTRY.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE'
    EXPORTING
      salesdocument    = ls_header_fields-vbeln
*     order_header_in  = ls_header
      order_header_inx = ls_order_header_inx
    TABLES
      return           = lt_return
      extensionin      = lt_bapiparex.

entry.title: Re: Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2508

When configuring pricing access sequences and related access tables in Public Cloud, it seems that this configuration is tenant-specific and not fully transportable across DEV, QAS and PRD systems.

Is there any recommended best practice to keep pricing access sequence configuration aligned across tenants?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Sap DataSphere local table partition size
entry.content:
👆

This is the correct answer.

For a primer on hashing, and hashes in general, you can check out this link

entry.title: Re: SMB Adapter for on-premise file share
entry.content:
Hello @djuggins43 / @fa1805 ,

SMB Adapter in SAP Integration Suite supports On premise connectivity via SAP Cloud Connector now, the update was released in August 2025.

More Details : SMB Adapter Feature: On-Premise Connectivity via SAP Cloud Connector 

 

Hope this helps!

Thanks.

entry.title: DO YOU NEED PUBLIC IP ON LINUX IN ORDER FOR BROWSER ACCESS NGINX CONFIGURATION TO WORK
entry.content: I have configured NGINX on the Windows server, and it successfully resolves browser access to the Windows server. However, during the SLD/authentication stage, the request is redirected to the Linux server’s internal IP. The NGINX reverse proxy configuration is attached. Is a public IP required on the Linux server?

entry.title: New Java Script Event in SAP Analytics Cloud End User File Upload
entry.content:
End User File Upload

In 2024 we shipped the end user file upload in SAP Analytics Cloud planning (see New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024). The feature has been well received by our customers and in widely adopted now. Since then we have been working on enhancing the feature (see New Clean and Replace Feature for SAC End User File Upload ) but also still see areas where we can improve, like the necessity to provide a more flexible un-pivoting (see also How to configure a dynamic file upload in SAP Analytics Cloud).

With version SAC QRC1 2026 we provide a new script event, that allows customers to check and massage the uploaded data before it is written into the system and to control error situations flexibly. 

Events in File Upload

So far we had two Java Script events in the file upload: onBeforeExecute and onAfterExecute. The event onBeforeExecute is executed before any of the upload functions is performed whereas onAfterExecute is triggered when everything has finished already. Customer have been asking for an additional event that is triggered during the upload. This event is now delivered with SAC QRC1 2026 and is called onBeforeImport.

 

When is the Event Called? Which information does it receive?

When running a file upload from the file upload starter the user is asked to select the file in a file selector. Once a file has been chosen the content of the file is uploaded to the browser. 

This is the moment the new event is triggered. In your script you have access to the following information:

  • file name of the chosen file (fileName),
  • the content of the file (fileContent),
  • the target version where the data should be written,
  • value of flag for using the first row as headers (firstRowAsHeaders).

The java script can return the object DataUploadFileHandler. In this object you can set two parameters:

  • continue
  • modifiedFileContent.

How can you react?

The first parameter enables you to abort the file upload. By default the value is set to 'true'. If you set the parameter continue to 'false', then the file upload will be stopped.

The second parameter can contain the modified file content. If you leave this parameter blank, then the system will use the originally uploaded data for further processing. In case the parameter is filled, the system will continue with the new content. That means that in your java script you have the option to not only check the uploaded data but even massage the data before further processing. For example, you could:

  • eliminate erroneous records,
  • correct erroneous records,
  • do dynamic un-pivoting, etc.

Please note that you will receive the data as uploaded with no mappings defined in data wrangling applied yet.

Provided that you do not abort the execution by setting the parameter continue to 'false' the system now proceeds with processing the data. The system will check whether a new file content has been provided - in this case it will proceed with the new content. If no new content has been provided, then the system proceeds with the unaltered initially uploaded content.

The data records are then sent to the data wrangling where all the mappings defined in the definition of the job are applied. Finally the data is checked and the correct data is written to the model.

Sample Coding

We want to give a simple example here. Let us assume we are uploading a file with header line (first row of the file consists of the column headers).

This is our sample coding in our script:

 
//1. send an information with the name of the uploaded file
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Warning, "Uploaded file name is" + fileName );
 
//2. show the content of the uploaded file in the console
for (var i=0;i<fileContent.length;i++){
var lineTest = fileContent[i];
console.log(lineTest);
}
 
//3. massage the uploaded file and only use the first 7 rows (including one header row) for the upload
var UpdatedFile = fileContent.splice(0,7);
var result = cast(Type.DataUploadFileHandler,{continue:true, modifiedFileContent:UpdatedFile});
return result;

In the first block we show an example, how to use the file name of the uploaded file and to display it to the end user /as a warning).

In the second block we show how to access the content of the uploaded file. We loop over the rows and display each row in the console.

In the last block we show how the uploaded data can be modified. We use a modified file content containing only the first 7 rows of the originally uploaded data. As the first row contains the header line we thus only us the first 6 data records from the original file.

Let us run the sample upload with the above script. We trigger the upload starter that is using our script. After our selected file is uploaded to the browser, we get the information which file has been selected.

 Next, we have a look at the console and can view the content of the uploaded data. Our uploaded file containes 10 data records.

And finally, once the upload has finished, we get the information that 6 new records have been written into the selected version.

Thus we can see that indeed the modified data was used that we have stripped down to 6 records (plus the header line).

entry.title: Re: Setting Up the Crossboarding Process with SAP SuccessFactors Recruitment Management
entry.content:
@SachinPachauri - regarding the section in blue below from your post - is it correct to say that in order for a rule to initiate (such as an onSave rule) during crossboarding specifically that only the "onboarding" context must be true while all others are false? So, we need to make a copy of ALL jobInfo and compInfo rules? I cannot find any KBA or documentation that asserts this other than this blog, and I find it strange that this is not explicitly stated in the admin guide, however I do see this behavior in our testing instance - rules that have onboarding context as "yes" with others as "yes" do not seem to trigger during crossboarding.

Note: Please make sure only the context of the “Onboarding” rule is true.

In all scenarios, we need to have the same rule twice. One with all other contexts as true and another one with the “Onboarding” rule context as true.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Deploy Joules Skills in SAP BPA - ClientNot...
entry.content: It seems the error - Client Not Found - is related to OAuth Authentication step...I never did this activation before but this kind of error could be something in the step related to some specifc call to access token. When you pass the wrong credentials, the call will fail. Just my 2 cents as the other error I see related to this is not having the necessary roles...but everyone here said that has the roles...so the error points to something different...the Client ID may be wrong...but just my 2 cents.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / Mass Maintenance of Sales Area Texts and Email Notes
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
i have to following two questions regarding the mass maintenance of sales Area Texts and Email Notes and i have combined them because they are related to the same tools:

1. My first questions is about the ability to add a new sales text to existing business partners.
My current workflow is the following:
I start by exporting the data for the relevant business partners including the table KNVV_STXL. In a second step i added a new row with the new text and used the action code I (Insert). The issue i faced is, if I try to import the file in the App F6553A i get a warning that "the Action Code I is not permitted for the table KNVV_STXL".

My question is, is there a another action code that can achieve this ? If not, what is an other way to add Sales Texts to business partners in mass ?

2. The second issue is related to the mass maintenance of email notes. In my research i have so far found out, that these notes are saved in the table ADRT and proably in the field AD_REMARK2. But from what i have seen so far, i am not able to export this table within the app Export Master data - business partners (F6551A). Therefore im also not sure if its possible to insert a new entry for this table with the MDG tools.

My question is, is this table and field out of scope for the MDG processes ? If so, is there an alternative way to maintain it in mass ?

Thank you for all responses
Best regards Lukas
